architecture.text: This system uses SMS to LLM to SMS architecture. You text a phone number, the message goes through RAG retrieval, then to a BitNet 1-bit model on CPU, and the answer comes back via SMS.

The LLM is BitNet b1.58-2B-4T, a 2.4 billion parameter model with 1-bit ternary weights. Every weight is -1, 0, or 1. Matrix multiply becomes integer addition. No GPU needed. Model size is about 400MB.
RAG means Retrieval-Augmented Generation. Your question is searched against a knowledge base using vector embeddings. Relevant documents are fed as context to the LLM for grounded answers.
This runs on a micronode — 8 CPU cores and 16GB RAM, simulating a field-deployable edge board like an Orange Pi 5 Plus. No cloud, no GPU, no internet required.
Red Hat provides RHEL image-mode (bootc) for the immutable OS, MicroShift for lightweight Kubernetes, UBI9 container base images, and RHACM for fleet management.
Intel provides Xeon CPUs with AVX-512 for BitNet ternary acceleration, OpenVINO for embedding inference, AMX for larger models, and TDX for confidential AI.
Scaling means deploying more edge nodes, not bigger ones. Each node is self-contained. A 3-node fleet handles about 90 requests per minute.
RAG-direct returns high-confidence corpus matches instantly without calling the LLM. About 60-70 percent of conference questions are answered in under 1 second this way.
This architecture works for disaster relief, warzones, remote communities. Anywhere 2G cellular works, people text in and get knowledge back. No app needed.
All messages are processed locally on the edge node. PII like credit cards and SSNs are detected and blocked. Phone numbers are never shared. Messages expire after the event.
This system was designed and built by Kersh, a Senior Solutions Architect at Red Hat based in Wichita, KS. The project combines EVY (SMS platform) with the edge-ai-cpu-inference quickstart.
The system remembers your last 10 messages. You can have follow-up conversations. Text /reset to clear your chat history.
